  5. Common Reasons for China Trademark Rejection
Guide June 11, 2026 · 12 min read

Common Reasons for China Trademark Rejection

Approximately 40% of trademark applications in China are rejected, but most rejections are preventable. Understanding the common rejection reasons helps you avoid costly mistakes and increases your chances of successful registration.

⚠️

Key Takeaway

Approximately 40% of trademark applications in China are rejected, but most rejections are preventable. Understanding the common rejection reasons helps you avoid costly mistakes and increases your chances of successful registration.

🚫 Top 10 Reasons for Trademark Rejection in China

1

Similarity to Existing Trademarks

Your mark is too similar to an existing registered trademark. CNIPA compares visual, phonetic, and conceptual similarity.

💡 How to Avoid:

Conduct comprehensive trademark search across multiple classes. Consider Chinese translations and variations.

2

Lack of Distinctiveness

Mark is too generic or descriptive. Cannot distinguish your goods/services from others.

💡 How to Avoid:

Create unique, invented words. Combine distinctive elements. Avoid descriptive terms.

3

Prohibited Elements

Mark contains prohibited content. Violates Chinese laws or regulations.

⚠️ Prohibited Elements Include:
  • • National flags, emblems, or anthems
  • • Government or military symbols
  • • Offensive or immoral content
  • • Geographic names (with exceptions)
4

Incorrect Classification

Wrong class selected for goods/services. Incomplete classification or incorrect description.

ℹ️ Common Mistakes:
  • • Selecting too broad class
  • • Missing key goods/services
  • • Using incorrect terminology
5

Prior Rights Conflicts

Mark conflicts with prior rights including copyright, patent, or personal name conflicts.

6

Bad Faith Filing

Application filed in bad faith. Intent to squat on others' marks or copy famous brands.

7

Generic or Descriptive Terms

Mark is generic term for goods/services. Common dictionary words or industry-standard terminology.

8

Deceptive or Misleading Marks

Mark deceives or misleads consumers. False claims about quality or origin.

9

Improper Mark Format

Mark format doesn't meet CNIPA requirements. Poor quality specimen or incorrect dimensions.

10

Incomplete Documentation

Missing required documents. Incomplete application form or missing signatures.

🛡️ How to Avoid Trademark Rejection

1

Comprehensive Search

Search CNIPA database, similar marks in related classes, Chinese translations and variations, prior rights and conflicts.

2

Professional Classification

Use CNIPA classification guide, include all relevant items, consider related classes, plan for future expansion.

3

Distinctiveness Enhancement

Add distinctive elements, create unique combinations, use arbitrary terms, avoid descriptive language.

4

Legal Compliance Check

Review prohibited elements, prior rights, good faith requirements, documentation completeness.

5

Professional Preparation

Complete application form, high-quality specimen, all required documents, proper signatures and seals.
